MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-18-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEFENDANTS 363 VALENCIA, LLC, BYODB LLC AND JOHN ELLSWORTH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED AS TO ALL THREE DEFENDANTS. THE COMPLAINT, WHICH FRAMES THIS MOTION, ALLEGES THAT ALL THREE MOVING DEFENDANTS ARE LIABLE BECAUSE THEY OWNED, OPERATED, AND/OR CONTROLLED UPTOWN AND/OR THE PREMISES WHERE PLAINTIFF EVA MUSGROVE WAS INJURED AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT. MOVING DEFENDANTS SATISFIED THEIR INITIAL SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN BY PROVIDING EVIDENCE THAT THEY DID NOT OWN, OPERATE OR CONTROL UPTOWN OR THE PREMISES AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PRESENTED ANY EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE DISPUTE ON THIS ISSUE. PLAINTIFFS' APPARENT EFFORT TO IMPOSE LIABILITY ON THE MOVING DEFENDANTS BASED ON SUCCESSOR LIABILITY OF SOME KIND FAILS BECAUSE SUCCESSOR LIABILITY IS NOT PLED IN THE COMPLAINT AND THUS IS OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF THIS MOTION. NOR HAVE PLAINTIFFS PRESENTED ANY FACTS TO SUPPORT SUCCESSOR LIABILITY AGAINST ANY OF THE MOVING DEFENDANTS. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING DEFENDANTS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G)AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR# 7489, MGHENO@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED. (D302/HK)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-01-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-31-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brittany Rogers, Esq. 415-544-0115 of Law Office of Scott Righthand for Plaintiff.
Robert Aaron, Esq. 415-438-7804 of Aaron & Wwilson LLP for Defendant through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues. Defendant opposes any extension of time. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT 363 VALENCIA, LLC, BYODB, LLC, JOHN ELLSWORTH, Alexander Rosenstein's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication.

Defendants 363 Valencia, LLC, BYODB LLC and John Ellsworth's motion for summary judgment is granted as to all three defendants. The complaint, which frames this motion, alleges that all three moving defendants are liable because they owned, operated, and/or controlled Uptown and/or the premises where plaintiff Eva Musgrove was injured at the time of the incident. Moving defendants satisfied their initial summary judgment burden by providing evidence that they did not own, operate or control Uptown or the premises at the time of the incident. Plaintiffs have not presented any evidence creating a triable dispute on this issue. Plaintiffs' apparent effort to impose liability on the moving defendants based on successor liability of some kind fails because successor liability is not pled in the complaint and thus is outside the scope of this motion. Nor have plaintiffs presented any facts to support successor liability against any of the moving defendants. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for the moving defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CCP 437c(g)and CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR# 7489, mgheno@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)
